paragraphs: So that was the threshold.[#pause] We're way past it now.

sections.heading:
32 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
HANGAR BAY 01, FNS BRISINGER

paragraphs.text: "[grave]We will not be docking with OC. That much is clear," Adrian Farley said.
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs.text: "[pressing]We're out of time," Stacey replied. "[pressing]If we're going, it has to be now. The Stingray fits a pilot, a co-pilot, and six in the back."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Carlson, Hague, and Farley were holding council with Kiran and Hale. In one click, the *Brisinger* would reach its designated orbital entry point above Mairee.
Marcus Carlson had already made peace with the cost.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]My duty is to this ship and her crew. Their well-being is my charge. I am staying."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs: All eyes turned to Adrian Farley. And he looked torn.

paragraphs.text: "[breaking]My daughter…[#pause] Sarah," he said, his voice breaking. "[breaking]She's at the Delta."
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: He shut his eyes, holding back his tears.

paragraphs.text: "[anguished]Every part of me wants to go to her."
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: He gazed up.

paragraphs.text: "[resigned]But I can't. Not yet. I will remain here as a liaison. You must reach her. Give her what she needs. Please."
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: Lena Hague placed the medical case by the others underneath the Stingray's belly.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]It's all there," she said. "[reassuring]I'll make sure she gets it. I'll wrap up my work here, four, five thuds."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: Alistair Hale nodded. "[focused]Alright, Stace, let's run the plan one more time."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[briefing]Phase one," Stacey began. "[briefing]We launch covertly. First, we go to O3 and get Lieutenant Muro before OC can intervene. Phase two, we head down to Freya Homestead to find the Oliver Savannen and the COG Egg. Phase three, we meet Farley's daughter at the Delta and start helping out the resistance however we can."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[wry]We'll be squeezed for space," Alistair said, eyeing the cases.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[measured]Not just that," Stacey said, looking at Alistair. "[measured]The Stingray might have some hull damage. Before we send it into Mairee's atmosphere, I need to run a stress diagnostic on the hull."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Alistair met Stacey's gaze, feeling the critical undertone.

paragraphs.text: "[clipped]Fine," he said. "[decisive]Let's take her out for a calibration run, right now."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: While the council continued with their preparations, Alistair [#bay-doors]undocked the Stingray from the *Brisinger*'s bay, moving free from the mothership's interference so they could perform the diagnosis.

paragraphs.text: "[focused]Alright, Abacus. Let's run the full sweep."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: They worked smoothly together. Stacey ran the internal diagnostic while the drones finished their exterior tests. As Stacey ran through the systems, Alistair put the ship through a series of short, sharp maneuvers: a roll to port, a brief burst from the vertical thrusters. He listened to the vibrations through the frame, searching for the slightest dissonance in the machine's harmony.
The final diagnostic confirmed the Stingray was ready to fall through Mairee's atmosphere.
An intimate silence filled the cockpit. Ahead, Mairee appeared. For the first time since they went through the Fold, she was no longer a distant speck but a stunning, living jewel.
Alistair ran a hand through his sweat-damped hair as he gazed out.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Well, hello there."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Stacey watched the swirling turquoise storms below.

paragraphs.text: "[awed]I've seen her projected a thousand times, but this…"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: She reached out, her fingers tracing Mairee's curve as if she could touch the world itself.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]Here she is for real."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Alistair watched Stacey. Awe shone on her face as the planet's light reflected in her eyes. He put his hand on her shoulder.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]We're on the shore of a new ocean, Abacus," he whispered.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]What if…[#pause] what if we really are cut off from Earth, Heyoka?" she asked.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: He squeezed her shoulder. "[wry]I guess you're stuck here with me."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: She put her hand on his.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Stace," he started, "[soft]about what's coming… I…"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[hushing]Shh," she said, stopping him.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: She unclipped her harness. She touched his cheek, her thumb tracing his jaw.

paragraphs.text: "[hushed]We don't have to talk about tomorrow. Not yet."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: He leaned into her touch and closed his eyes. When he opened them, he examined the high collar of her flight suit.

paragraphs.text: "[playful]That magnetic zipper," he said with a grin, "[playful]is a marvel of engineering. But it's definitely in the way."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: She smiled. "[teasing]Is that a complaint, pilot?"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[mock-serious]It's a request for a diagnostic," he said, grabbing the zipper. "[mock-serious]I need to…[#pause] check the, uh, structural integrity."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He unzipped the suit, revealing her black compression shirt underneath.
She let him continue. Her hands went to the seals on his suit to open them.
They slid their suits down. Soft blue light outlined their bodies, showing the curve of his shoulder and her neck. He rested his forehead against hers.
She pulled back a little.

paragraphs.text: "[fragile]I'm afraid," she confessed.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Me too."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: His breath warmed her lips. He kissed her, then moved his lips along her jaw to her ear. She tilted her head, exposing her neck.

paragraphs.text: "[reverent]You're phenomenal in this light."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: His fine hairs rose at her touch. Stacey traced his arm to his chest, and his muscles tensed. She reached his flight suit at his hip and pulled it farther down.
Alistair helped her take it off. His thumb followed the line of her shorts.
She shivered. She kissed him again and ran her fingers through his hair.
He glanced past her, out the viewport.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]If this is the last shore we ever reach," he said, "[steady]I want to explore all of it with you."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Stacey didn't answer. She climbed onto him. Her palm went from his hair down his chest, tracing his ribs and abs.
He pulled the hem of her shirt. She arched her back in agreement, and he slid the shirt up and over her head. He massaged her breasts. A soft, shuddering moan escaped her lips.

paragraphs.text: "[breathless]Heyoka, whatever happens tomorrow…"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[low]I'm not making any promises I can't keep."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He kissed the hollow of her throat.

paragraphs.text: Stacey drew back. "[searching]Who are you when you're not flying, Alistair Hale?"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: He went quiet, his hand stroking her hair. "[soft]Flying?"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Just…[#pause] from one place or person to the next." She searched his face. "[gentle]What happens when it catches up?"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: All bravado was gone.

paragraphs.text: "[bare]It already has," he said, looking past her at the planet outside. "[bare]Look at it, Stace. We are at the edge of everything."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He reached up and brushed a strand of hair from her cheek.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]So whatever happens down there tomorrow…[#pause] I'm with you. That's my mission."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: She leaned in and kissed him slowly. He held her. Between the *Brisinger* and the alien home below, within the star-filled void, they came together as the Stingray, on autopilot, turned about.

The murals along the corridor pulsed in response to her passage, recognizing Lena as one of their own. She arrived at the Med-Bay. The operating table was still there, but it was empty, though the smell lingered. Lena took it all in with a detached interest. Her eyes scanned the room. Dangling her legs on a bed at the far end of the Med-Bay was a blue-haired young woman. She was softly singing a song.

paragraphs.text: "[measured]It is time," Lena said as she strode toward her, "[measured]to make your choice."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs: The girl did not reply. She simply tilted her head in acknowledgment and held out her arm. Lena took a hypo-pen from her case. Its contents shimmered: a mix of enzymes and custom phages to speed up her transformation.
She pressed the pen to the girl's inner arm until it [#hypohiss]hissed. The girl did not flinch.
She accepted Lena's blessing. Her blue irises swirled with a new light. They met with Lena's, and she received her thoughts.

paragraphs.text: *You carry the seed. Find the boy. The stream flows with you.*

paragraphs: Her eyes fluttered as the inoculant took effect.
Lena packed her instruments and three canisters into a sterile case. Her work finished, she left the Med-Bay. The girl's humming faded behind her.
Meanwhile, as the little Colors mingled during their diagnostic run, inside the small, barricaded storage room, the Seiðrs inside Adrian Farley and Marcus Carlson conspired.

paragraphs.text: "[melodic]Our pretty cultivator is preparing the seedling," Marcus said with a melodic murmur.
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: "[melodic]The girl. Her roots will find fertile ground."
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs.text: "[melodic]But the ground there still yearns for the rain." Marcus sighed, his gaze distant.
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs: Adrian nodded, his eyes closed as if in prayer. He opened them. The irises swirled like a galaxy in miniature. An understanding passed between them.

paragraphs.text: "[melodic]But your little Color must guide them down," Marcus continued, his voice dropping, becoming more intense. "[melodic]Deep down, when the time is right."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: "[melodic]My daughter," Adrian added, "[melodic]she needs to attune first."
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: His eyes shot past Marcus.
Adrian closed his eyes again, concentrating, and a shadow passed over his face.

paragraphs.text: "[unsettled]I heard a wrong Note."
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: A few moments passed with his eyes still closed, then they blinked open.

paragraphs.text: "[shaken]And again! It can't be. It sounded like…"
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs.text: "[warning]Shhh. Don't mention her," Marcus warned. "[grim]She's made her choice."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

sections.heading: HANGAR BAY 01, FNS BRISINGER
paragraphs: The Stingray returned from its diagnostic run, still humming from the flight. In the dim cockpit, Alistair and Stacey took their time resetting their consoles, worried their cheeks would betray a warmth that hadn't belonged to the flight.
When boots sounded on the grated deck, they glanced down as Lena approached.

paragraphs.text: "[brisk]Everything check out?" Lena asked, her voice cutting through the hum.
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: "[steady]All clear," Alistair said.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[approving]Good," Lena said.
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs: She set her case down and locked it with a few [#clicks]clicks.
A single light by the hatch turned green.

paragraphs.text: Stacey called out, "[ready]Good to go again!"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: Alistair Hale took one last look around the bay. "[checking]All set?" he asked Lena.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Lena nodded. She got in and settled in a rear seat as Alistair [#hatch]pressed the hatch controls.

paragraphs.text: "[clear]Bay doors opening," Stacey called.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: The hull [#bay-doors]groaned as the heavy panel slid aside, showing the star-filled space beyond.

paragraphs.text: "[focused]Course plotted. First stop: Orbital Three."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: The Stingray's drive started with a [#thrust]rising whine, pushing the crew into their seats as the ship fell forward into the dark.

paragraphs.text: "[easy]So," Alistair said once they were clear, "[easy]weirdest Solac story. Go."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: Stacey snorted, a small, unexpected sound. "[incredulous]Seriously? That's your icebreaker?"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[teasing]Hey, we've got a long way to fall," he countered. "[teasing]Either this, or I'm loading the karaoke."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[sighing]Fine," Stacey sighed and thought for a moment. "[deadpan]Fucking. Ensign. Popa," she said, emphasizing each word. "[recounting]Dude forgot to replace the vacuum seal on the latrine before cycling it. Guess who earned the nickname 'Pooper'?"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: Alistair laughed. "[laughing]No way. What a shitface."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[disgusted]Ugh," Lena grunted, her face twisted. "[recounting]Alright, I got one. A young cadet, barely eighteen. He comes into the infirmary. Actually, 'comes in' isn't right. He hobbles in, pale as a sheet, green around the gills, and clutching himself like he's trying to keep his guts from spilling out."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs: Alistair and Stacey turned to face Lena, sensing a good story.

paragraphs.text: "[continuing]He could barely speak through the pain, just kept muttering about a 'steam-scrubber malfunction.' I get him on a bed, I lower his pants, and the problem is…[#pause] immediately obvious. One of his testicles is the size and color of a plum."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: "[wincing]Ouch," Alistair winced, rubbing his legs.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[clinical]Ouch doesn't begin to cover it," Lena said. "[clinical]It's a classic case of testicular torsion. That means the spermatic cord is twisted. This cuts off the blood supply. You got four thuds to treat it before the patient loses its…[#pause] uh…[#pause] 'payload'."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: "[curious]How in the void would a scrubber cause that?" Stacey asked with a mask of horrified curiosity.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[mock-grave]That," Lena said, "[mock-grave]is the best part. He'd been trying to 'recalibrate' his personal steam-scrubber. Apparently, he'd found a way to bypass the safety limiters on the 'drying cycle'. You know that jet of warm air?"
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs: She looked from Alistair to Stacey, her expression deadpan.

paragraphs.text: "[deadpan]He had successfully managed to increase the oscillation frequency to a…[#pause] 'pleasurable' level. The problem was, after a pulse, the drying cycle automatically switched to the 'scrub' cycle."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: "[alarmed]Oh, no." Alistair's eyes widened.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[deadpan]Oh, yes," Lena confirmed. "[deadpan]Running way beyond maximum intensity…"
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs: Alistair and Stacey held their breath.

paragraphs.text: "[proud]I managed to save the testicle," Lena finished with a note of professional pride.
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs: Alistair sank back in his gel-couch, shifting his legs.

paragraphs.text: "[hoarse]Okay," Alistair said, a little hoarse. He cleared his throat. "[hoarse]Right. That's…[#pause] yeah."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He fidgeted with a strap on his harness, unable to find a comfortable position.

paragraphs.text: "[conceding]I think you win, Doc. I got nothing that can top…[#pause] that."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[wicked]Come on, Heyoka," Stacey prompted with a wicked grin. "[wicked]You can't back out now. Pay up."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[grunting]Fine," Alistair said with a grunt. He rubbed his face, as if trying to erase the mental image Lena had expertly painted. "[reluctant]Alright."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[suspicious]This isn't going to be about you, is it?" Stacey asked.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[stammering]As if!" he stammered. "[recounting]Okay, so a pilot cadet I once knew was up for a drunken dare."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He paused, a look of fond, remembered idiocy on his face.

paragraphs.text: "[recounting]The dare was this: that he'd 'tag' the viewport of the station commander's personal quarters with a can of spray paint by taking an EVA pod for a hike. So, this drunken idiot steals a pod and heads out. He reaches the commander's window with a can of bright yellow paint in the grabber arm."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He started to laugh, the memory still vivid.

paragraphs.text: "[laughing]The idiot actually did it. He sprayed…" He started to laugh again, "[laughing]he sprayed 'HI MOM!'"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: The cockpit went quiet again. "[huffing]Really?!" Stacey huffed.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[delighted]Oh, yes," Alistair confirmed, wiping a tear from his eye.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[asking]Did he get away with it?"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[mock-grave]He got expelled, of course," Alistair said.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He fixed his gaze on Stacey and winked.

paragraphs.text: Stacey punched him amicably. "[laughing]Shit, no. That was you!"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

sections.heading:
32 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
ORBITAL COMMAND

paragraphs.text: "[low]There she is."
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs: On the holoscreen, the interceptor probe's feed sharpened: the FNS *Brisinger*.

Eva Bellamy recognized the moment for what it was — a reset of the board. She loosened her stance. Ulre stood beside her, rigid at the console, hands clasped. Behind the tactical display, the compact man looked even smaller than he had a moment before.

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]Probing," an officer reported.
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs: The image became clearer. The probe's floodlights moved over the ship, and everyone [#gasp]gasped. Glowing, organic matter was smeared across the viewports. Beautiful, alien, and unreal.

paragraphs.text: "[snapping]What is that mess?" Ulre snapped. "[snapping]Boarding solution, now!"
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs.text: "[clipped]Comms?" Eva queried.
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]Probe is relaying short-range hails on all spectra," the officer replied. "[reporting]No response."
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs.text: "[pressing]Still nothing?"
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]Nothing but static," the officer said, adjusting the filters. "[reporting]A low-frequency hum."
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs: A flicker of unease passed between Eva and Ulre.

paragraphs.text: "[clipped]Vanguard?" Eva asked.
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]Vanguard reports all bays are unresponsive. They are locked out."
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs: Ulre slammed his fist on the console.

paragraphs.text: "[furious]Locked out?! Then punch your way in!"
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: The officer hesitated, glancing first at Ulre's furious face, then to Eva for instruction, shaking his head as he did.

paragraphs.text: "[icy]Director," Eva said, voice icy. "[icy]We can't board them."
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs: Her console buzzed, indicating the *Brisinger* had sailed past its designated deceleration point.

paragraphs.text: Ulre turned on her, his teeth bared. "[furious]Why not?!"
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: She looked at the telemetry.

paragraphs.text: "[tight]The Bris is past the deceleration point. She's in the gravity well. It's one thing for Vanguard to match her vector without melting its thrusters," she said, voice tight but controlled. "[tight]It's another to have her cut her way into the Bris. There's no stable solution that doesn't spike thermal load past tolerance."
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: Ulre didn't back down. "[defiant]I've run hotter."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs.text: "[firm]She's already inside the gradient."
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: "[barking]Tow her out with the Trjegul," Ulre barked.
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs.text: "[sneering]No!" Eva sneered. "[sharp]We agreed that risk is unacceptable."
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: Ulre slammed his console harder. "[yelling]We agreed to stop her!" he yelled.
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: Eva pressed her lips into a hard line. This was not leadership but a tantrum. Cold pragmatism had enabled him up until now. She had enabled him. The realization made her nauseous. She inhaled sharply, purging emotion.

paragraphs.text: "[calm]Too late. What is her trajectory?" she asked, calm and controlled.
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]Heat shielding is angled perfectly," the officer replied. "[reporting]Someone is flying her."
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs: He brought up the projected flight path, a red line plunging toward the planet.

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]Direct descent to Freya Port."
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs.text: "[murmuring]She's bypassing orbital audit," Eva murmured. "[low]Full speed. Hot entry."
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs: The bridge erupted in a babble of frantic reports.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Silence!" Ulre screamed. "[shouting]We've been duped! Rebels!"
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: He spun, wild-eyed.

paragraphs.text: "[barking]Centurion! Get your men to the Depeche. We need to get down there before the Bris. Move!"
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: He stormed off, the Centurion in tow.

paragraphs.text: *Thank the void.*

paragraphs: Eva thought, as the tension on the bridge eased the moment he left. She turned to the screen and calculated burn rates. The *Brisinger* began a smooth descent. As it reached the upper atmosphere, friction and fire made it appear to have glowing wings.
A sharp [#alarm]tactical alert drew her gaze to a secondary monitor. A high-velocity intercept vector had appeared near Orbital Three. The tag flickered: *Stingray.*
She sent a warning to it and followed up by rerouting the Vanguard. As she did, a weight shifted in her chest.

paragraphs.text: *Muro. They're picking up Muro.*

paragraphs: The Vanguard was closing in on the unresponsive Stingray fast.
Eva bit her nail.
The Vanguard [#comms-beep]hailed her.

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][clipped]OC, this is Vanguard. Target is noncompliant. Requesting authorization to target drive plumes. Minimal chance of loss of life."
paragraphs.speaker: tom-keller

paragraphs: Eva thought of Muro, losing his mind, confined, and shifted her stance. She pictured it all too clearly, a mind eating itself. The nights she couldn't sleep because her thoughts wouldn't stop replaying themselves. The locked door. The terror of needing it. *For your own protection.*

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][pressing]Controller, what is your call?" the pilot of the Vanguard pressed.
paragraphs.speaker: tom-keller

sections.heading: STINGRAY

paragraphs.text: "[strained]We're—pain—ting—a—tar—get!" Stacey stammered against the G's.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[strained]Let—'em—look," Alistair replied, still pushing the burn.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: The main comms channel [#comms-beep]beeped a direct hail from Orbital Command.
< OC | You are on an unauthorized vector.
Cut your thrust.
State your intentions. />

paragraphs.text: "[wry]Not—asking—nicely."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Alistair reached for the gimbal lock. Then came the flip. The Stingray groaned as it thrust its way to match the station's trajectory. The crew groaned too. The spidery comms array appeared as a fly on the windshield.

paragraphs.text: "[grim]We have a problem," Stacey said grimly while her stomach churned. "[grim]I'm hailing Lieutenant Muro, but he's not responding. The habitat clamps are cold."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: It had been Muro's call. No hard dock. Too many variables, too much time they didn't have.

paragraphs.text: "[remembered]Just get me close," he'd said. "[remembered]I'll make the jump."
paragraphs.speaker: frank-muro

paragraphs: Now the success of the operation depended entirely on a man who was currently demonstrating a remarkable lack of participation.
Alistair scanned the schematic.

paragraphs.text: "[focused]There. The emergency EVA hatch on the habitat module is hot."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: The console [#comms-beep2]beeped again, this time with a more aggressive tone.

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]We have a dance partner inbound," Stacey reported.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: < Vanguard | Stingray.
That you, Heyoka?
Rattle on your tail here.
Let's keep it friendly between us. />
*Rattle.* Alistair knew Tom Keller as a smooth guy. Smooth hair, uniform. Smooth talk, especially. And Alistair was not in the mood for it.

paragraphs.text: "[focused]Abacus, I need a perfect firing solution to get our belly lined up with the hatch. Can you punch in the numbers?"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Reaching the hatch was one thing. Getting Muro inside the Stingray was another. They couldn't make a hard dock. The best he could do was hold the Stingray close, keeping its open belly a dozen meters from the hatch. Muro would have to jump.

paragraphs.text: Stacey bit her lip. "[reporting]Solution ready."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Alistair gazed out the viewport to see the lights from the approaching Vanguard.

paragraphs.text: "[urgent]It's now or abort!"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: Stacey hailed again. "[surprised]Still nothing. Wait. I'm receiving!"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][bright]Hi, there!" Frank said. "[over comms][bright]I am ready to jump!"
paragraphs.speaker: frank-muro

paragraphs.text: "[relayed]He's ready," Stacey shared. "[focused]Toss the mag-rope."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Alistair's eyes flicked to the viewport, locking onto the approaching thruster flare.
< Vanguard | Hey. Hale.
Your window is closing.
Don't make me spin you. />

sections.heading: ORBITAL THREE, “THE EARS”

paragraphs.text: *THEY'RE CALLING.*

paragraphs: Frank Muro struggled to get into the emergency void suit. He had been fumbling with it for five pulses.

paragraphs.text: *My hands…[#pause] they feel like somebody else's.*
*OUR HANDS,* another voice inside replied. It was Gary's voice.

paragraphs: Frank, with his arms stuck in the unsealed suit, pushed clumsily toward the comm.

paragraphs.text: "[strained]I cannae reach it."
paragraphs.speaker: frank-muro

paragraphs: The comm [#comms-beep]chirped again, even louder. He sealed the suit's torso, twisted the helmet into place, and locked his boots to the deck by the EVA hatch.
He lingered on his nest.

paragraphs.text: "[breaking]This is it, Gary," he whispered. "[breaking]I have to leave you now."
paragraphs.speaker: frank-muro

paragraphs.text: "[shrieking]NO!" Gary shrieked. "[shrieking]You cannae leave me. I'm you! I'm next."
paragraphs.speaker: gary

paragraphs: The CIX's panic matched his own fear of abandonment. Tears sprang to his eyes.

paragraphs.text: "[breaking]I have to. I have ta leave ya here, dear friend."
paragraphs.speaker: frank-muro

paragraphs: Gary's voice shifted. It repeated his own thought back to him.

paragraphs.text: "[wistful]Ye've been a dear friend."
paragraphs.speaker: gary

paragraphs: The handheld comm [#comms-beep2]chirped a third time, even more insistent. Frank finally answered the comm.

paragraphs.text: "[bright]Hi, there!" Frank said with an unusual cheer. "[bright]I am ready to jump!"
paragraphs.speaker: frank-muro

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][steady]Copy that." Kiran's voice crackled back. "[over comms][focused]We have a solution for the hatch. Just catch the mag-rope. You can jump."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: With three pulls, the handles released. The [#decompression]hatch slid open, and violent decompression pulled him into space. He tumbled through a star-dusted rabbit hole, toward the Stingray's open belly. The mag-rope found him.

Inside Orbital Three, the CIX cube was knocked out by the decompression. It too floated away from its console and drifted through the hatch. Its eye pulsed red as it tumbled. It was still tethered by an umbilical, so it stayed there.
Frank felt an inner tearing. His 'Self' split apart. His body tumbled before him.

paragraphs.text: *Gary?* The thought was a whimper. *Gary…[#pause] where did I go? It's dark. Please…[#pause] please…[#pause] where am I?!*

paragraphs: He watched a man in a voidsuit being pulled into the Stingray's belly. The hatch closed, sealing the man inside.

paragraphs.text: *Wait…[#pause] that's no' me. I'm still here!*

paragraphs: The Stingray veered off, accelerating toward the planet below.
A cube spun in the void. Just a little box. Below, the horizon of Mairee turned — a staircase infinitely winding down.

paragraphs.text: *What a downshot, Gary.*

paragraphs: That was the box's thought as it blipped into oblivion.

sections.heading: ORBITAL COMMAND
paragraphs: Eva keyed the mic. Her hand was steady.

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][resolute]Negative, Vanguard," she said. "[over comms][resolute]I repeat. Disengage, let them go. We don't fight our own."
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs.text: Her comms officer gasped. "[disbelieving]Controller!" he yelled with disbelief. "[disbelieving]Contact! I have contact! Brisinger. It's the Brisinger!"
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs.text: "[urgent]On screen. Now!"
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs: His trembling finger tapped the main holoscreen on.
There, as if glued into a swirling mural, appeared Captain Raj Gupta. Smiling.

paragraphs.text: "[serene]Little Colors. I have brought you a canvas. Let's paint!"
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs.text: "[shocked]Captain Gupta?!" Eva said, shocked.
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs: Her mouth stayed open.

paragraphs.text: "[serene]It's time you opened your gift," he said.
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs.text: "[confused]What are you talking about?"
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs: Eva held out her hand — realizing she was holding something. An old watch, of sorts. She opened the casing and noticed that the inner workings were not steel gears but tiny insects. Segmented backs glistening, legs hooking into one another, mandibles catching and releasing in rhythmic clicks. They turned each other the way teeth should turn cogs. When one snagged, another forced it forward. When one tore, the rest adjusted. She understood then, with a cold, terrifying clarity, the grinding of life against life. With that realization, she remembered a nursery rhyme.

lines: the bee wriggled to be free,
the stickier stuck he would be.
So it shot out its stinger,
the only way it could poke.

paragraphs: That's how she understood the struggle would never stop. Not unless…
She took a step toward the holoscreen. The bridge lights flickered in time with her pulse.

paragraphs.text: "[serene]You feel it, don't you?" Gupta said. "[serene]The lie we tell ourselves. That there is space. That there is time."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: Eva swallowed.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]This isn't cruelty," Gupta went on gently. "[gentle]There is no ill intent."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs.text: "[realizing]The Fold," Eva answered. "[realizing]Within, we were nudged against something. Another —"
paragraphs.speaker: eva-bellamy

paragraphs: Gupta nodded, and a ripple spread through his mural.

paragraphs.text: "[resonant]Skin? Another being? In another dimension? We need room," Gupta boomed. "[resonant]Room to wiggle. Like that little speck inside you."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: Gupta's image shifted. As the *Brisinger* struck the thermosphere, friction lit the viewport, illuminating Gupta and his mural. His skin turned pearlescent.

The projector died. A prickle climbed her wrist, light and deliberate. She jerked her hand, but the sensation followed up the tendon, over the vein, beneath the sleeve. Tiny points catching, releasing. Climbing. Eva gazed down at the open watch and noticed the escaping insects crawling on her forearm. Gooseflesh rose in a spreading wave. She brushed at it, sharp and frantic. Still, the crawling continued. Up. Inward.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Controller!"
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs: The voice slapped her awake.

She gasped. Her fingers closed around her wrist. The watch and insects were gone. Her other hand found her belly, and she held it there. She could tell she was still on the bridge as a wave of vertigo and nausea overtook her. She felt the supporting grip of her comms officer as she fought not to collapse.
